entries.decision: Ship lume via the plugin's bin/ directory (stdlib python3 shim), no pip/pyproject for the plugin install path.
entries.rationale: bin/ is auto-added to PATH while the plugin is enabled, so lume resolves as a bare command with zero runtime deps - satisfies stdlib-only and 'install must itself be simple'. A pyproject/console_script path can be published later for non-plugin users; not required by done-when.

entries.decision: Fuller .claude-plugin/plugin.json: name, explicit version (0.1.0), description, author, repository, license, keywords.
entries.rationale: Explicit version pins releases rather than riding the git-SHA-as-version default; the metadata orients a stranger and the marketplace listing.

entries.decision: Move the engine to the repo root (./lume package + ./bin/lume + ./.claude-plugin/). .lume/ becomes in-repo operator state ONLY and is NOT part of the plugin/package.
entries.rationale: Operator decision: root-level is the idiomatic plugin layout a stranger expects; the engine should not live nested under a runtime-state dotfolder. .lume/ is functionally outside the plugin - plugin.json never references it. Execution must keep this behaviour-preserving (227 tests + our own dogfooding loop) and confirm the mechanism by which .lume/workstreams does not ship as plugin content.

entries.decision: Expose the loop as a small set of namespaced skills (SKILL.md) that call the bundled lume by bare name and relay --json; not one raw command per verb.
entries.rationale: Wrapper not rewrite: skills map onto existing verbs via the self-describing catalog, and a guiding skill ('you are at handback; next is accept') serves a new operator better than thin per-verb commands.

entries.decision: Install = marketplace add + /plugin install (with --plugin-dir for local dev); lume seed is the operator's first step on their own repo; updates stay explicit - no daemon, no background upgrade.
entries.rationale: One-time operator-run action per the non-negotiables; explicitly does not adopt mcp-cli's self-driving/daemon axis.

entries.decision: Root README hand-written for what/why/install/iteration-loop/first-command; the command list is derived from the verb catalog rather than hand-maintained.
entries.rationale: A stranger needs orientation prose the catalog cannot give; the mechanical verb list should not drift from lume verbs.

entries.decision: Three execution slices: (E1) move engine to root + plugin skeleton; (E2) skill/command wrappers over verbs; (E3) root README + install path + clean-repo smoke test.
entries.rationale: F3 (the move) gates everything and carries the largest blast radius, so it lands first and stays behaviour-preserving; wrappers then docs build on a resolving package.
